revision.comment: undid revision [[Special:Diff/1256201297|1256201297]] by [[Special:Contributions/94.50.125.85|94.50.125.85]] ([[User talk:94.50.125.85|talk]]) fiction and science fantasy are different things
revision.text:
[[Anthropomorphism]] is the attribution of [[human]] traits, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities. "Anthropomorphism" comes from two Greek words, ανθρωπος, ''anthrōpos'', meaning human, and μορφη, ''morphē'', meaning form. {{Commons cat|Anthropomorphism}}
[[Category:Human body]] [[Category:Human body in popular culture]] [[Category:Fiction]] [[Category:Science fiction themes]] [[Category:Transhumanism]] [[Category:Narratology]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Mass importing wikipedia categories, not taking histories because these are unused ones and it is kind of obvious where they came from
revision.text:
[[Anthropomorphism]] is the attribution of [[human]] traits, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities. "Anthropomorphism" comes from two Greek words, ανθρωπος, ''anthrōpos'', meaning human, and μορφη, ''morphē'', meaning form. {{Commons cat|Anthropomorphism}}
[[Category:Human body]] [[Category:Human body in popular culture]] [[Category:Fiction]] [[Category:Science fiction themes]] [[Category:Transhumanism]] [[Category:Narratology]]

revision.comment: fixing the uncategorized categories error
revision.text:
[[Anthropomorphism]] is the attribution of [[human]] traits, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities. "Anthropomorphism" comes from two Greek words, ανθρωπος, ''anthrōpos'', meaning human, and μορφη, ''morphē'', meaning form. {{Commons cat|Anthropomorphism}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
[[Anthropomorphism]] is the attribution of [[human]] traits, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities. "Anthropomorphism" comes from two Greek words, ανθρωπος, ''anthrōpos'', meaning human, and μορφη, ''morphē'', meaning form. {{Commons cat|Anthropomorphism}}[[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]]
[[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Human body]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Human body in popular culture]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Fiction]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Science fiction themes]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Transhumanism]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Uncategorized categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Narratology]] [[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:Unused categories]][[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates. [[{page.name}]]
revision.text:
[[Anthropomorphism]] is the attribution of [[human]] traits, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities. "Anthropomorphism" comes from two Greek words, ανθρωπος, ''anthrōpos'', meaning human, and μορφη, ''morphē'', meaning form. {{Commons cat|Anthropomorphism}}
